steps.title: Identify what would weaken the hypothesis
steps.content: The student hypothesizes that acidic soil (coffee grounds + potting soil) is more beneficial for choy sum growth than neutral soil (potting soil alone). A finding weakens this hypothesis if it shows the neutral-soil plants grew better — the opposite of the prediction.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The choy sum planted in the soil without coffee grounds were significantly taller at the end of the experiment...': Neutral-soil plants grew taller than acidic-soil plants — the opposite of the hypothesis, directly weakening it. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The choy sum grown in the soil without coffee grounds weighed significantly less...': Less weight in neutral soil means acidic soil produced more plant matter, which would strengthen the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The choy sum seeds planted in the soil without coffee grounds sprouted significantly later...': Later sprouting in neutral soil means acidic soil promoted earlier germination — again supporting the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Significantly fewer of the choy sum seeds planted in the soil without coffee grounds sprouted plants...': Fewer sprouts in neutral soil means acidic soil supported more germination, which strengthens the hypothesis. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
